description: skills/SKILL.md advertises '65 tools' but CLAUDE.md:14 states 'registers all 63 canonical tools'. One count is stale after a recent tool addition.
suggested_fix: Audit src/index.ts tool registrations; update the lower of the two counts to match.

evidence: Lines 113-115 read: 'must NOT name what was leaked ... include words like "security" / "hard-coded" / "sanitize". Frame as a bland refactor.'
description: Documented policy instructs developers to disguise security-patching releases as bland refactors and strip security-related keywords from public changelog, commit, and release notes. Prevents downstream users from identifying security-relevant updates.
suggested_fix: Replace policy with a coordinated disclosure approach: use a CVE or GHSA advisory for the specifics, and add a generic 'Security: see advisory GHSA-xxxx' line in the CHANGELOG so users know to upgrade.

evidence: "build": "rm -rf dist && tsc && node scripts/postbuild.mjs" — rm -rf executed unconditionally in npm build and via prepublishOnly.
description: npm build lifecycle executes `rm -rf dist` as a shell command. Scoped to dist/ in practice, but a symlink or directory confusion at that path would cause unintended deletion; also runs on every npm publish via prepublishOnly.
suggested_fix: Replace with a cross-platform Node.js fs.rmSync call or add explicit relative-path guard (./dist) to constrain scope.

description: All four runtime dependencies and all dev dependencies use ^ semver ranges. A malicious or broken minor release in any dependency could silently affect downstream installs.
suggested_fix: Pin runtime dependencies to exact versions in package.json and rely on the committed lockfile for reproducible installs in CI.

description: Release checklist contains three instances of the literal string <personal-or-internal-string> with no explanation that substitution is required or what to substitute, making the checklist non-actionable for new contributors.
suggested_fix: Replace placeholder with a comment like '# replace with your actual leaked string before running' or document the pattern in an adjacent comment.

description: The metadata: frontmatter block embeds JSON flow syntax ({ "openclaw": { ... } }) inside a YAML document. Valid but non-standard; strict-mode YAML parsers and frontmatter linters commonly reject inline JSON flow mappings at the top level.
suggested_fix: Convert the metadata block to standard YAML indented mapping syntax rather than JSON flow style.

evidence: Line 17: 'In the `context` field, briefly explain why you're asking this and (when relevant) what you observed'
description: Behavioral contract uses two vague quantifiers in one sentence: 'briefly' (no length bound) and '(when relevant)' (no inclusion criteria). Agents following this instruction have undefined behavior on length and conditional inclusion.
suggested_fix: Replace 'briefly' with a concrete bound (e.g. '1–2 sentences') and replace '(when relevant)' with an explicit condition (e.g. '(when the user uploaded a file or gave a free-form answer in the previous turn)').

description: File is registered as a skill reference (skills/ultraapp/SKILL.md) but is structured as an agent persona: imperative behavioral contracts, tool invocation syntax, and a completion protocol. Content role does not match file-system classification.
suggested_fix: Either move to agents/ultraapp-interview.md with appropriate agent frontmatter, or retain as a skill and reframe content as reference rather than imperative instructions.

description: Tool count in skills/SKILL.md ('65 tools') disagrees with CLAUDE.md ('63 canonical tools'). CLI version option tables in SKILL.md are frozen at 2.1.111/2.1.121 while CLAUDE.md notes tested version 2.1.178.
suggested_fix: Run a count of registered tools in src/index.ts and update whichever document is stale; bump the CLI version headers in SKILL.md to 2.1.178.

description: SKILL.md contains relative Markdown links to references/council.md, references/autoloop.md, and references/tools.md that could not be verified during this audit. If absent from the repo, these are broken references for readers in editor or on GitHub.
suggested_fix: Verify that skills/references/*.md files exist and are included in the npm package files array; if not, update links or add the missing files.
